source_context:
CV Assistant uses llmfit for hardware-aware model selection — automatically
profiling the local machine and ranking compatible AI models by fit score.

Step 1 — Hardware Probe:
The probe_hardware tool calls the llmfit CLI to inspect the local system.
It captures: CPU model and core count, total RAM (GB), GPU/NPU VRAM (GB),
accelerator type (Metal / CUDA / CPU), and OS platform.
Example output for Apple M4 Max: 14 cores, 36 GB RAM, 36 GB VRAM, Metal GPU.

Step 2 — Capability Scoring:
llmfit scores each candidate model against the hardware profile.
Scoring factors: model parameter count vs available VRAM, quantization level
(Q4/Q8/F16), context window requirements, and inference speed estimate (tok/s).
Models are ranked by a composite fit score (0–100).

Step 3 — Ranked Recommendations:
The system produces a ranked list with fit scores, e.g.:
  qwen3.5:latest         → score 94  (fits in VRAM, fast inference)
  qwen3-coder:30b        → score 81  (fits with Q4 quantization)
  qwen2.5-coder:latest   → score 78  (lighter, very fast)
  llava:34b              → score 52  (marginal VRAM fit)
  mixtral:8x7b           → score 31  (too large for available VRAM)

Step 4 — Auto-Selection & Agent Initialization:
The highest-scoring available model is auto-selected.
The agent is initialized with create_agent(model=selected_model).
The selected model is displayed in the Web UI sidebar footer.
Users can override by setting LLM_MODEL in .env.

Step 5 — Model Pull (if needed):
If the selected model is not yet downloaded, the agent triggers
ollama pull <model> automatically before starting inference.
Pull progress is streamed via SSE to the Models view in the UI.
communicative_intent: Hardware-aware model selection: llmfit probes local hardware (M4 Max, 36 GB VRAM), scores and ranks candidate models, auto-selects the best fit, and initializes the agent.
